reviews.review_msg: We were supposed to have two classes per week with him. He divided our class in 4 groups, and each group had 15/20min class per week with him. However, he just sent us YouTube videos to watch before class and we only discussed our questions. In other words, he did not teach us anything, he only answered questions

By far the worst instructor I have ever had the displeasure of dealing with at this institution thus far throughout my entire undergraduate career. He does not care about your grade or if you even learn anything, just that he is right and you are wrong. Very inflexible and does not have any concern towards any of the students.

He is setting you up to do poorly. Lectures are extremely boring and he seems like he has little experience. Entire grade based mainly on midterm and final which had failing averages after he gave everyone 5 extra points. There are 3 essays and I don't know anyone who even received a passing grade on them. Not sure how were going to pass.
Erik makes tough exams. lectures and ppt aren't really helpful. Gotta study all by yourself.
test very long can't even finish class average is not even 50%

His lectures are read straight from his powerpoints, which I found to be unhelpful and is delivered in a monotone pitch. During class discussions he will put you down in front of the class which is unprofessional and shows a lack of constructive teaching ability. In reference to the very unstructured class project do not expect helpful feedback.
very smart guy, 5 random quiz per semester but it isn't that hard. Exam and Mid term are base on the lecture note.
Avoid him. random pop quiz + hard exam

He is a tough grader. However, if you are interested in the course and ready to learn, he is a great option. He is knowledgeable in experimental economics,  knows the material very well and can be inspirational in experimental design. Never expect to sleep the course over and simply get a pass. Overall, he is one of my favourite!

He gives simple analogies about concepts, and solves the math problems step by step without skipping anything. If you're bad at calculus, it doesn't matter who the professor is, you'll find 201 hard, but Erik teaches it really well. His quizzes are an easy 20% of the grade and his tests are really fair.

He is not helpful at all. He criticizes project proposals but offer no help on what he thinks is the right track to follow. He talks too fast and I understand less than 5% of everything he says. He does not even seem to care if students understand him or not. Please avoid him.
Read the textbook beforehand, then you will understand whats going on in class. Go to the tutorials, then you will know whats going on during your mid-term & finals.

His lectures help if you read the textbook beforehand; regardless, the material is pretty challenging for a 200-level course. The math knowledge required transcends Calc I - if you study hard, know at least Calc II math, and go to ProfTA's office hours, you might have a chance at an A. Don't slack, pop quizzes  20%; hard MT (40%) - need intuition
bad prof!

He is very nice but not really clear with explaining the materials. He made pretty hard mt and final. Since his lecture notes is not very clear and he has no sample tests, it became really hard to prepare for exams. I barely studied the whole course by reading the textbook myself.

He is really nice and helpful and he explains the materials quite clearly. The materials discussed in class are not very helpful for the project which determines your final score. If you are interested in the topic, you can really learn something from the course.
All the material he goes though he doesn't explain well at all! Most of the students have no idea whats going on and he doesn't care. I would avoid him if possible

he is pretty good, the lectures are easy, and midterm with final mostly from them. the book you can read for additional information, and the workbook for practice which is not that hard. the books are expensive, but you can find both of the books online and for free. the random quizzes i thought would be bad, but they are pretty easy and worth 5%.
Erik is awesome. Comments above are not very accurate. Econ301 or 201 is not an easy course. You don't expect to get a fairly good grade unless you really try your best.

His class is just HARD, especially for those people who are not good at math.  Econ 301 is purelly a math class.  Random in class quiz is stupid and his lecture seems easy but exam is very tricky.  Beware about him

In general, he is not a bed person. However, he doesn't know how to grade fairly. He was picking about the grammar mistake and sentence structure on the group paper. As a ECON class, that's kinna silly. Also, he lack of the experience to tech in SFU since he was a fresh noob while I taking the class.
